_meta.note: agno uses useFrontendTool (Strategy B) for HITL — produces frontend-tool-call shapes, not LangGraph interrupt() events

fixtures._comment: gen-ui-interrupt sales-call pill — first leg: schedule_meeting frontend tool call. Agno's HITL uses useFrontendTool, so the tool call triggers the TimePickerCard via the frontend tool pipeline. The probe asserts time-picker-card testid mounts.

response.content: Sure — let me check available times.

fixtures._comment: gen-ui-interrupt sales-call pill — second leg: confirmation after user picks a slot and the tool result is fed back.

response.content: Booked: Sales intro call confirmed for the slot you picked.

fixtures._comment: gen-ui-interrupt alice-1on1 pill — first leg: schedule_meeting frontend tool call.

response.content: Got it — pulling up next-week slots.

fixtures._comment: gen-ui-interrupt alice-1on1 pill — second leg: confirmation after user picks a slot.

response.content: Scheduled: 1:1 with Alice locked in for the slot you picked.
